narrative_focus: fragility-first structural review with honest caveats about the current radius machinery

scripts_used.purpose: Validate the checked-in Python implementation at suite scope.
Exercise the end-to-end pipeline on the checked-in quickstart example.
Run the repaired published-DAG-style sweep and inspect fragility rankings.
Run a tiny synthetic witness that currently yields a certified radius of 1.

commands_run.result: Completed in 1.57s; returned radius [0,0]; emitted top fragility scores 0.7900 / 0.7375 / 0.3150; baseline estimation failed on continuous labels.
11/11 DAGs completed in 22.6s total; all radii [0,0]; no ATEs; nontrivial fragility rankings surfaced, including A->U2 at 0.8950 and Sprinkler->Rain at 0.8250.
Generated benchmarks/live_fragility_recovery_results.json with a certified radius of 1 on a 3-node chain example; solver=fpt; top fragility edge=[0,2]; top fragility score=0.525.

grounded_claims.claim: The checked-in Python implementation is substantial and currently passes the full automated test suite.
The end-to-end quickstart path runs successfully and emits nontrivial fragility rankings, even though the radius result is currently degenerate and baseline estimation fails on continuous labels.
The repaired published-DAG-style sweep completes on all 11 built-in examples and surfaces high-impact directed pairs, but every run still returns radius [0,0] and no ATE.
A tiny live synthetic example currently yields a certified radius of 1, confirming that the radius machinery can succeed on at least small cases.
The most honest current positioning is fragility-first structural review rather than a validated benchmark paper on minimum-edit robustness radii.

removed_claims: Broad benchmark-superiority narratives around robustness radii.
Citation-heavy groundings that were not tied to directly rerun code paths.
